apiErgonomics:
VERDICT: The happy path genuinely delivers the 'AR ergonomics without AR traps' promise; the edges ship several real AR traps. Evidence from F:/Replica/NAS/Files/repo/github/sylin-org/koan-framework/src/Koan.Data.Core/Model/Entity.cs (948 lines, 84 'public' tokens), EntityContext.cs, Data.cs (575 lines), AggregateExtensions.cs.

WINS: (1) `Todo.Get(id)` returns nullable, `todo.Save()`, `Todo.Query(x=>...)` lower into a unified Filter AST with one execution path (Data.cs:73-142 — plan/pushdown/residual centralized, adapters carry no fallback logic — genuinely good architecture). (2) Awaitable `Todo.Count` accessor (Entity.cs:155-185) — `await Todo.Count` defaults to Optimized, `.Exact()/.Fast()/.Where()` explicit; clever, discoverable. (3) Lazy GUID v7 on string-key Id (Entity.cs:927-941). (4) Capability probe-by-cast `Data<T,K>.As<TCapability>()` (Data.cs:44) is clean. (5) RemoveByQuery rename (Entity.cs:393-402) shows the author learning: XML doc candidly records that `Entity.Remove(id)` for string TKey silently resolved to the query overload and threw NotSupportedException — proof the overload matrix has already bitten in production, fixed by verb-explicit naming.

TRAPS/BURDEN: (1) IntelliSense on `Todo.` shows ~60 statics: Get x4, All x4, AllWithCount, Query x7 (LINQ/JSON-DSL/QueryDefinition/sort combos), QueryWithCount x3, QueryRaw, AllStream x3, QueryStream x4, FirstPage x3, Page x3, Count, Batch, Copy/Move/Mirror x3 each, Upsert x2, UpsertMany x2, Remove x6, RemoveByQuery x2, RemoveAll x3, Patch, PatchMerge, Events, GetParent x3, GetParents, GetChildren x3, GetRelatives, SupportsFastRemove — plus 8 Save/Upsert extension overloads. Partition-as-trailing-string multiplies every verb; the file itself admits string-sort overloads were abandoned because they collide with partition overloads (Entity.cs:143-144 comment). (2) Verb aliasing: Save IS Upsert (AggregateExtensions.cs:29-33 'Alias for Upsert'), entity says Remove while facade says Delete, `Query(string)` is the JSON DSL while QueryRaw is provider-native, Jobs pillar uses `.Where()` where Data uses `Query` — four naming dialects. (3) `Get(ids)` returns IReadOnlyList<TEntity?> (nulls interleaved). (4) Relationship navigation is a shipped AR trap: GetChildren/GetRelatives load ALL rows and filter in memory — Entity.cs:843 comment 'For now, load all children and filter in memory'; GetParent returns Task<object> and `return null!` (Entity.cs:586); reflection-based Data<,> dispatch (Entity.cs:783-897) in the flagship type. (5) AsyncLocal ambient context: EntityContext.cs:112 doc says 'Replaces any previous context (does not merge)' but lines 140-146 inherit prev Source/Adapter/Partition/CacheBehavior — doc/code contradiction in the same file; TransactionScope.Dispose does sync-over-async Commit/Rollback then `catch { }` (EntityContext.cs:295-304); good call though: dispose-without-commit defaults to ROLLBACK matching .NET TransactionScope. (6) Lazy Id getter mints identity on read — serializing an unsaved template object assigns it an id. SCORE 3.5: top-decile happy path, but the god-file, overload matrix, in-memory relationship loads, and ambient-context doc drift are exactly the AR traps the promise disclaims.
failureExperience:
VERDICT: Fail-fast is NOT real at boot; it is fail-at-first-use with two redeeming qualities (good resolution-failure messages, critical health probes) undermined by a boot pipeline that structurally swallows its own failures.

THE SMOKING GUN — F:/Replica/NAS/Files/repo/github/sylin-org/koan-framework/src/Koan.Core/Hosting/Bootstrap/AppBootstrapper.cs:110-124: the AddKoan() module-initializer loop wraps every IKoanInitializer.Initialize in `catch { // best-effort to avoid failing the host during bootstrap }` — no logger, no console, nothing. A module that throws during registration silently vanishes; the developer discovers it at first use as 'Unable to resolve service'. Same pattern at AppRuntime.cs:130-133 (per-registrar Describe swallowed), AppRuntime.cs:109 (the ENTIRE boot report render in `catch { /* best-effort */ }` — failures of the failure-reporting are silent), AppRuntime.cs:166 (`try { /* intentional no-op */ } catch { }` — cargo-cult), StartupProbeService.cs:60 (outer `catch { }` around probe broadcast).

MISCONFIGURED PROVIDER (wrong conn string): nothing fails at boot. PostgresHealthContributor (IsCritical=true) runs in a fire-and-forget Task.Run, marks 'data:postgres' Unhealthy with raw Npgsql ex.Message in the health snapshot — host starts anyway; IsCritical only becomes a 'critical=true' fact string. First use: RepositoryFacade calls EnsureReady which throws raw NpgsqlException (logged LogWarning then rethrown, PostgresRepository.cs:208-224). No Koan-level wrapping, no 'check Koan:Data:postgres:ConnectionString' hint at the throw site. Config typos compound it: AdapterConnectionResolver.cs:76-113 has three nested `catch { /* fall through */ }` on Convert.ChangeType — a malformed setting silently degrades to the default value.

GOOD MESSAGES (when resolution itself fails): missing connection string throws listing the three exact config keys to set (AdapterConnectionResolver.cs:55-58); no adapter package: 'No IDataAdapterFactory instances registered. Ensure adapter packages are referenced and AddKoan() has been called.' (AdapterResolver.cs:76-78); unknown source names the exact config path (AdapterResolver.cs:40-47); cache adapter missing names the package pattern (CacheAdapterResolver.cs:34). No-AddKoan: 'AppHost.Current is not set. Ensure services.AddKoan() and greenfield boot (AppHost.Current + IAppRuntime).' (Data.cs:26) — actionable first half, internal jargon second half. There is NO KoanServiceDoctor or equivalent diagnostic — grep finds zero hits.

CENSUS: 243 empty/comment-only catch blocks across 93 files in src/ (plus 136 catch-blocks that only return/continue, 78 files). Caveat for fairness: a minority are defensible — typed capability fallbacks (`catch (NotSupportedException) { /* fall back */ }` Data.cs:292,316), `ObjectDisposedException` in Jobs (JobOrchestrator.cs:94), diagnostics-only provenance in Cache registrar. But the untyped catch-all-without-log pattern dominates the BOOT path itself and the SQLite adapter — exactly where a 'self-reporting infrastructure' framework can least afford it. SCORE 2: excellent resolution-failure messages can't compensate for a bootstrap that eats module failures unlogged and provider misconfiguration that surfaces as a raw driver exception minutes after a 'successful' start.
bootReport:
VERDICT: Real and better than typical .NET console noise, but materially less than CLAUDE.md's 'Self-Reporting Infrastructure' claim — the rich provenance is collected, then mostly not shown.

WHAT A DEVELOPER ACTUALLY SEES (KoanConsoleBlocks.cs:16-102, rendered by AppRuntime.Discover via Koan.Data.Core/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs:94 and KoanWebStartupFilter.cs:34): a boxed `┌─ [KOAN] Application` block with Name/Code/Environment(+OrchestrationMode)/Host/Session/Runtime-version, a Registry section (initializer counts by namespace, auto-registrar/background-service/adapter counts), an Inventory section (module name → version, deduplicated), an Environment section (InContainer/Process/Uptime/Machine/Health), plus `[K:PHASE] warmup(...)→registry(...)→data(...)→services(...)→ready(...)` timing and a 'Koan Ready' block with URLs. That is genuinely informative for a v0.x framework.

GAPS: (1) The per-setting provenance system (ProvenanceRegistry, ProvenanceModuleWriter with sources/states/secret-redaction via Koan.Core.Redaction — well-designed, src/Koan.Core/Provenance/) is NOT in the boot output; only module name+version surfaces. Settings/decisions are exposed only through Koan.Web.Admin status endpoints and the OpenAPI transformer — so 'services describe their capabilities in structured boot reports' is half-true: they describe, the boot report doesn't print it. (2) The Health line races its own data: StartupProbeService probes in a background Task.Run while the block prints at Discover() — typically 'probes=0 overall=Unknown' at the moment a developer reads it. (3) Raw `Console.WriteLine` pollution bypassing the logger: 'ASSEMBLIES|loaded=...' plus a raw JSON serialized assembly-scan event (AppBootstrapper.cs:151-170) — machine format interleaved with human format on stdout. (4) The whole render is wrapped in `catch { /* best-effort */ }` (AppRuntime.cs:109) — if report-building throws you silently get no report. (5) Failed module initializers are invisible in the report (swallowed upstream), so the Inventory can show a module as present whose registration partially failed. SCORE 3.5: above-average reality, oversold claim.
debuggability:
VERDICT: Build/symbols plumbing is professional; the exception model and debugging documentation are not.

GOOD: Directory.Build.props:14-18,40-41,56-57 — ContinuousIntegrationBuild=true default, PublishRepositoryUrl, EmbedUntrackedSources, IncludeSymbols + snupkg, Microsoft.SourceLink.GitHub 8.0.0 — correct, complete SourceLink setup (value capped externally by stale 0.8.x nuget.org packages). Logging defaults (Koan.Core/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs:37-59): custom KoanConsoleFormatter with short categories + source suffix, Debug minimum outside Production, Microsoft/System filtered to Warning — sane, dev-friendly.

BAD: (1) NO exception hierarchy — zero hits for KoanException; grep finds ~27 scattered one-off *Exception types, mostly in experimental pillars (Secrets, Rag, Media, Vector); the data core throws generic InvalidOperationException/NotSupportedException and lets raw provider exceptions (NpgsqlException etc.) escape at first use. A developer cannot `catch (KoanDataException)` or filter Koan failures from app failures. (2) TWO parallel console formatters: public 'Koan' (KoanLogFormatter via AddKoanFormatter) vs internal 'KoanConsole' (KoanConsoleFormatter, what AddKoan actually wires) — plus a README_MIGRATION.md in the Logging folder; mid-renovation residue. (3) Debugging docs are drifted shells: docs/guides/troubleshooting/{adapter-connection-issues,bootstrap-failures}.md are deprecation stubs (status: deprecated, framework_version v0.6.2) redirecting to docs/support/troubleshooting.md which is frozen at v0.6.3 / 2025-09-28, instructs grepping for `Koan:modules` / `Koan:discover` log markers that do not exist in the current boxed-block output, and references the Flow pillar. (4) .claude/skills/debugging/SKILL.md teaches `Describe(BootReport report, ...)` but the real interface is `Describe(ProvenanceModuleWriter module, ...)` (src/Koan.Core/IKoanAutoRegistrar.cs) — the skill's canonical code sample doesn't compile against current source; its 'expected success patterns' show the same nonexistent `Koan:` log lines. (5) KoanLog static-bridge pattern (KoanLogFactoryBridge hosted service) means pre-host log calls can be lost or console-fallback formatted. SCORE 2.5: symbols/logging foundation earns 4, exception model and doc accuracy earn 1.5-2.
swallowCensus: TOTALS (empirical, rg multiline over src/, pattern = catch with empty or comment-only body): 243 sites / 93 files; additional catch{return-or-continue-only} sites: 136 / 78 files

By project: Koan.Core 29; Koan.Orchestration.Cli.Core 27 (condemned ARCH-0077 stack); Connectors/Data/Sqlite 23; Koan.Orchestration.Generators 19; Koan.Data.Core 11; Connectors/Data/Postgres 10; Koan.Web 9; Koan.Storage 9; Koan.Mcp 9; Connectors/Data/SqlServer 9; Connectors/Data/Mongo 8; Koan.Web.Auth 7; Koan.Cache 6; Connectors/Orchestration/Docker 6; Koan.Jobs 5; remainder 1-5 each across ~30 projects

WORST: F:/Replica/NAS/Files/repo/github/sylin-org/koan-framework/src/Koan.Core/Hosting/Bootstrap/AppBootstrapper.cs:120-123 — AddKoan initializer loop swallows EVERY module registration failure with zero logging ('best-effort to avoid failing the host during bootstrap'); also :60, :72, :92, :95, :238 (assembly scan + manifest reflection, unlogged)

F:/Replica/NAS/Files/repo/github/sylin-org/koan-framework/src/Koan.Core/Hosting/Runtime/AppRuntime.cs:109 — entire boot-report render in catch{/*best-effort*/}; :130-133 per-registrar Describe swallowed; :166 'try { /* intentional no-op */ } catch { }'

F:/Replica/NAS/Files/repo/github/sylin-org/koan-framework/src/Connectors/Data/Sqlite/SqliteRepository.cs — 23 sites (lines 89, 143, 161, 185, 201, 242, 247, 302, 352, 615, 936, 955, 1007, 1009, 1048, 1071, 1192, 1209, 1218, 1335, 1358, 1469, 1482): cache-invalidation, path-resolution, DDL/column-validation catches — settled-core adapter, worst single file

F:/Replica/NAS/Files/repo/github/sylin-org/koan-framework/src/Koan.Data.Core/AdapterConnectionResolver.cs:80, :95, :110 — Convert.ChangeType failures silently fall through config priority levels: a typo'd setting value silently becomes the default

F:/Replica/NAS/Files/repo/github/sylin-org/koan-framework/src/Koan.Data.Core/EntityContext.cs:300-304 — TransactionScope.Dispose swallows Commit/Rollback failure (sync-over-async GetResult then catch{})
F:/Replica/NAS/Files/repo/github/sylin-org/koan-framework/src/Koan.Core/StartupProbeService.cs:60 — outer catch{} around the whole startup health-probe broadcast
F:/Replica/NAS/Files/repo/github/sylin-org/koan-framework/src/Koan.Web/Hosting/KoanWebStartupFilter.cs:32 (+3 more) — web boot path KoanEnv init swallowed

FAIRNESS NOTE: census regex also matches defensible typed catches — e.g. Data.cs:292/316 catch(NotSupportedException){/*fall back*/} capability fallbacks, JobOrchestrator.cs:94 catch(ObjectDisposedException), Koan.Cache/Initialization/KoanAutoRegistrar.cs:95-98/:125 diagnostics-only provenance. Roughly estimated 20-25% of sites are typed/benign; the dominant pattern is untyped catch-all with no logging, concentrated on the boot path
